nysed-ela-2024-g3-mc-q13.alt: Question 13. The phrase “change before your very eyes” in paragraph 8 suggests that clouds Choices: A: can only be seen up close B: can look strange to others C: can make it difficult for you to see D: can become different while you watch

nysed-ela-2024-g3-mc-q14.alt: Question 14. What claim does the author make about cumulus clouds? Choices: A: They look like bright cotton balls in the sky. B: They form only when it is windy for many hours. C: They show that the weather might soon change. D: They look pink when they form at sunset.

nysed-ela-2024-g3-mc-q15.alt: Question 15. In which section of the passage can the reader find information about how clouds are made? Choices: A: “Tiny, Floating Drops” B: “Changing Clouds” C: “Types of Clouds” D: “Study the Clouds”

nysed-ela-2024-g3-mc-q16.alt: Question 16. Which sentence from the passage shows a cause-and-effect relationship? Choices: A: “Clouds can change shapes and sizes.” (paragraph 9) B: “Other clouds float in the middle, and some move up and down.” (paragraph 14) C: “As the day warms up, fog clears away.” (paragraph 17) D: “Scientists study clouds to tell what kind of day you’ll have tomorrow.” (paragraph 28)

nysed-ela-2024-g3-mc-q17.alt: Question 17. Which detail best shows how the author feels about clouds? Choices: A: “Some clouds travel as fast as race cars!” (paragraph 11) B: “You can barely see the road ahead of you!” (paragraph 15) C: “... cloudy nights are a little bit warmer than clear...” (paragraph 29) D: “... you are sure to find many wonderful shapes...” (paragraph 31)

nysed-ela-2024-g3-mc-q18.alt: Question 18. Which detail would be most important to include in a summary of the passage? Choices: A: Some clouds can look like shapes of animals. B: Clouds form when drops of water come together. C: Clouds can appear on warm days and on cool days. D: Birds fly through clouds that are low in the sky.

nysed-ela-2024-g3-mc-q19.alt: Question 19. Which event would be most important to include in a summary of the story? Choices: A: A man takes care of the lighthouse and uses a small key to start its light. B: A boat greets the lighthouse in the day but the lighthouse does not answer. C: A terrible storm comes in the night and the bridge reminds the lighthouse why it is needed. D: A group of workers come to the Hudson River and begin digging and building along the shore.

nysed-ela-2024-g3-mc-q20.alt: Question 20. How are the details in paragraphs 1 through 6 important to the story? Choices: A: The paragraphs explain the main problem to be solved in the story. B: The paragraphs introduce the setting and the main character of the story. C: The paragraphs show why later events will come to happen in the story. D: The paragraphs hint at how the characters will change over time in the story.

nysed-ela-2024-g3-mc-q21.alt: Question 21. How does the building of the great gray bridge affect the little red lighthouse? Choices: A: The bridge makes the lighthouse feel like it is not useful anymore. B: The bridge makes the lighthouse feel happy to have help with its job. C: The bridge shows the lighthouse new ways to help the people of the city. D: The bridge blocks out the light of the lighthouse so it is not seen by boats.

nysed-ela-2024-g3-mc-q22.alt: Question 22. The great gray bridge believes that the little red lighthouse Choices: A: can help it send lights into the air B: should be moved to a different place C: is too small to be helpful in an emergency D: must continue to do its job for the boats

nysed-ela-2024-g3-mc-q23.alt: Question 23. Lighthouses are important because they guide boats. They can help keep boats safe when weather is bad or water is dangerous. Which detail best shows how this idea connects to the events in the story? Choices: A: “All the way from Lake Tear of the Clouds, high up in the mountains, came the Hudson River.” (paragraph 3) B: “Now the boats on the river talked to the little red lighthouse as they passed.” (paragraph 4) C: “He took off the thick white cap that let it sleep by day. He turned on the gas with a funny small black key.” (paragraph 7) D: “The boats saw the light and were safe. The boats saw it, and they kept to the channel.” (paragraph 12)

nysed-ela-2024-g3-mc-q26.alt: Question 26. Which sentence best shows a theme of the story? Choices: A: Goals can be reached through hard work. B: Good things happen to people who tell the truth. C: Trusting what others do can be rewarding. D: The happiest moments in life are those spent with family.

nysed-ela-2024-g3-mc-q27.alt: Question 27. What does the word “muses” mean as it is used in paragraph 2? Choices: A: feels strange B: becomes amazed C: thinks deeply D: feels upset

nysed-ela-2024-g3-mc-q28.alt: Question 28. In paragraph 3 of the story, whose thoughts are being shown? Choices: A: Sam’s B: Molly’s C: Dad’s D: Mom’s

nysed-ela-2024-g3-mc-q29.alt: Question 29. Read these sentences from paragraph 20. Molly screws up her mouth. She sticks out her tongue to taste just a little. Which sentence best explains why Molly acts this way? Choices: A: She is certain that the applesauce will win the blue ribbon. B: She thinks that the applesauce will taste bad. C: She is angry at Sam for putting his apples in the applesauce. D: She wants to try the applesauce before Sam tries it.

nysed-ela-2024-g3-mc-q30.alt: Question 30. Which quote from the story best shows that Molly has learned a lesson? Choices: A: “Molly sighs. She hopes Sam will stay out of the way.” (paragraph 10) B: “‘They’re beauties,’ Mom says, and Molly smiles with pride.” (paragraph 12) C: “Then she gobbles the whole bite. ‘Wow!’” (paragraph 20) D: “We may even have a chance at that blue ribbon this year.” (paragraph 21)

nysed-ela-2024-g3-mc-q31.alt: Question 31. Which sentence best shows how Molly’s feelings about Sam change in the story? Choices: A: “It’s Sam, her messy little cousin who lives next door.” (paragraph 3) B: “‘Go pick apples over there,’ Molly snaps.” (paragraph 6) C: “‘No!’ Molly cries, reaching into the pot to pull out Sam’s apples.” (paragraph 14) D: “Who would have known that you had the missing ingredient?” (paragraph 22)
